method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — queensda.org/robots.txt disallows nothing). Fingerprinted queensda.org: WordPress 7.0.1 on Kinsta managed hosting behind Cloudflare, Divi theme with a 'queensdistrictattorney' child theme, Beaver Builder page builder (bb-plugin / bb-theme / bbpowerpack), WPML multilingual, Yoast SEO, Google Site Kit, FacetWP faceted search, and self-hosted Matomo analytics. Confirmed ZERO NYC Open Data assets via the Socrata Discovery API across four agency-label spellings (all resultSetSize 0). Enumerated the site's content through the fully-exposed WordPress REST API (wp-json/wp/v2): 1,557 posts, 60 pages, and the full category taxonomy with counts.
headline_findings: Queens DA is a new distinct pattern: it publishes prolifically (1,557 WordPress posts, 1,216 press releases) and that content is ALREADY machine-readable — but through an ACCIDENTAL API (the fully-exposed WordPress REST API, wp-json/wp/v2), not a designed one.
There is NO NYC Open Data corpus for a District Attorney — verified zero Socrata datasets across four agency labels. DAs are county/state prosecutorial agencies and do not publish to NYC Open Data.

The prosecution lifecycle (arraignment 131 → charges 127 → indictment 119 → court-case 378 → conviction) exists only as press-release prose tagged by WordPress category; there is no structured Case resource.

The one genuinely structured dataset is trapped in prose: the cold-case / unidentified-persons initiative encodes NamUs case numbers, sex, age range, and date/location found inside post TITLES (e.g. 'MALE 4-12-2014 NAMUS UP13060').
There is NO inbound write channel at all — tips, cold-case leads, and FOIL records requests go by phone or a static contact page. That is the net-new WRITE surface.
All five NYC borough DA offices run the same functions on similar WordPress stacks — this is the strongest candidate yet for ONE shared, five-borough DA API rather than five accidental REST APIs.

apis_observed.note: The WordPress REST API is fully exposed: 1,557 posts, 60 pages, categories, tags, media. Machine-readable but undesigned, uncontracted, and unofficial — press releases, court-case posts, and cold-case posts all readable as JSON. The de facto Queens DA API.
FacetWP powers on-site faceted search; its /refresh and /fetch endpoints are exposed under wp-json.
Self-hosted Matomo (first-party analytics) integrated into WordPress — notable choice for a public agency over pure third-party tracking.
WPML drives the multilingual site (translation state for 15+ strings); explains per-language content.
Google Site Kit and Yoast SEO REST namespaces present — third-party analytics/SEO tooling.
server: cloudflare; x-kinsta-cache / ki-edge headers; x-redirect-by: WordPress. Managed WordPress on Kinsta behind Cloudflare.
NO Queens DA presence — zero datasets across four agency-label queries. DAs do not publish to NYC Open Data.
Freedom of Information Law requests are the formal data-access path; handled manually via the Resources page. No API.

fruit.name: Press Releases
fruit.notes: The office's dominant output. Readable as JSON via wp-json/wp/v2/posts?categories=16 but with no designed contract — HTML narrative only. The core content object.

fruit.name: Prosecution activity (public record)
fruit.notes: The arraignment→charges→indictment→court-case→conviction lifecycle exists ONLY as press-release prose tagged by WordPress category. No structured Case resource; charges, dispositions, and sentences are unstructured text. Public record only.

fruit.name: Cold Cases / Unidentified Persons (NamUs)
fruit.type: structured data trapped in post titles
fruit.machine_readable: recoverable (NamUs ID, sex, age, date in titles)
fruit.notes: The one genuinely structured dataset: each post title encodes a NamUs case number, sex, estimated age range, and date/location found (e.g. 'MALE 4-12-2014 NAMUS UP13060'). Highest-value read; a family or agent cannot query it today.

fruit.name: Community Programs & Initiatives
fruit.count: ~dozen (A Bridge to Change, Brave Justice, Blazing the Trail, youth development, violence prevention, some archived RFPs)
fruit.notes: Diversion, youth, and community-partnership programs, each a bespoke page with no common shape. Some are archived RFPs.

fruit.name: Resources (FOIL, Property Release, CIU, helplines)
fruit.count: Conviction Integrity Unit, Property Release, FOIL, helpline lists, language/disability access plans
fruit.notes: Public resources as prose links on one page. FOIL is the formal records-request path — manual, no API.

fruit.name: Victim / Witness Services
fruit.notes: Survivor support (advocacy, safety planning, compensation, court accompaniment, interpreters) scattered across pages with no directory. Describes services, not people.

fruit.name: Submit a tip / cold-case lead / FOIL request
fruit.notes: There is NO inbound write channel — tips, leads, and FOIL requests go by phone or a static contact page. The net-new WRITE surface; tips may be anonymous, FOIL requests generally are not.

meta.platform: WordPress 7.0.1 (Divi + Beaver Builder + WPML) on Kinsta behind Cloudflare; FacetWP search; self-hosted Matomo
meta.netnew: TipSubmission — submit a tip, cold-case lead, or FOIL request (no inbound channel today)
meta.tagline: A prosecutor's office that publishes 1,200+ press releases and, by accident, already has an API — WordPress REST — but no designed contract, no open data, and no way in.
meta.headline: Queens DA already exposes a machine-readable API by accident (WordPress REST); the work is to STRUCTURE its press-release prose into real resources and share one contract across all five borough DAs.

scorecard_notes.platform: Modern managed WordPress (Divi/Beaver/WPML) on Kinsta behind Cloudflare — capable, current, but a CMS not a data platform.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: A live, fully-exposed WordPress REST API — real and machine-readable, but accidental, undesigned, and uncontracted; plus FacetWP fetch.
scorecard_notes.openData: Zero — no NYC Open Data / Socrata presence at all; verified across four agency labels.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Prosecution lifecycle is only prose tagged by category; the one structured dataset (cold cases / NamUs) is trapped in post titles.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: None — no inbound tip, lead, or FOIL channel beyond phone and a static contact page.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface; nothing designed for tools/MCP despite the content being JSON-readable.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Content and infra are owned (self-hosted Matomo is a plus), but the 'API' is an unmanaged side effect of WordPress, and analytics/SEO lean on Google.
